jokes.text: Can't believe Michael Bay spent a billion dollars of his own money to build that for The Rock.
jokes.setup: Andy sees Alcatraz and needs to comment on it
jokes.punchline: He thinks Michael Bay personally funded building the real Alcatraz for his movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - confusing reality with movies in a very specific, character-driven way that's both absurd and believable for him

jokes.text: I couldn't tell, 'cause you had that mask thing over your eyes.
jokes.setup: Andy recited an entire movie on the plane
jokes.punchline: He couldn't tell if Ben was sleeping because of a sleep mask
jokes.explanation: Solid Andy moment showing his obliviousness to social cues

jokes.text: What happens at a National Parks Conference is compiled and emailed to you in a PDF the following Monday.
jokes.setup: Leslie starts to quote 'What happens in Vegas stays in Vegas'
jokes.punchline: Subverts it with bureaucratic reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of famous phrase with perfect Leslie-style government worker specificity

jokes.text: They give tours. Yeah. That's exactly how they'll be expecting me to try to break in.
jokes.setup: Ben suggests tours to see Alcatraz
jokes.punchline: Andy treats this like a heist movie where tours are too obvious
jokes.explanation: Andy's movie-logic applied to normal situations

jokes.text: Wow. I've never worked hard on anything. What a cool life!
jokes.setup: Tom reflects on working harder than ever before
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's never worked hard on anything and celebrates it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment of self-awareness that somehow becomes self-congratulation

jokes.text: From now on, everyone call me Kristen. Because I am wigging out right now!
jokes.setup: Tom is stressed about the soft opening
jokes.punchline: Makes a wig/Kristen pun about wigging out
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom wordplay, a bit forced but in character

jokes.text: They're not even done painting that sign. It says 'Tom's bi.'
jokes.setup: Tom mentions the unfinished restaurant sign
jokes.punchline: The incomplete painting creates an unintended message
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup that's economical and gets a solid laugh

jokes.text: Liam Bonneville? The Department of the Interior's resident bad boy?
jokes.setup: Grant mentions Liam Bonneville
jokes.punchline: Leslie calls a bureaucrat a 'bad boy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie enthusiasm applied to the most mundane government figure possible

jokes.text: No idea.
jokes.setup: Andy asks what Gryzzl does
jokes.punchline: Ben admits complete ignorance despite pitching to them
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that reveals the absurdity of tech companies

jokes.text: Six months ago, I was working in a taco truck. Now, I own one-third of the Portland Trailblazers.
jokes.setup: Tech executive explains Gryzzl's success
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous rags-to-riches story typical of tech bros
jokes.explanation: Good satire of Silicon Valley success stories

jokes.text: In fact, a lot of people go to the library just to order pizza online.
jokes.setup: Ben boasts about Pawnee's online pizza ordering
jokes.punchline: Reveals people need to go to library for internet
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that undercuts Ben's boasting with Pawnee's technological limitations

jokes.text: Sure, obviously, we're no Akron. I mean, we're more like Dayton. But with your help, we can become Toledo.
jokes.setup: Ben tries to position Pawnee competitively
jokes.punchline: His aspirational comparison cities are all equally unremarkable
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation where each city mentioned is equally unglamorous

jokes.text: Ben, we don't like to say the word 'no'... No. ...at this company.
jokes.setup: Executive explains they don't like saying no
jokes.punchline: Says no in the middle of explaining they don't say no
jokes.explanation: Good structural joke with immediate contradiction

jokes.text: So instead, let me say, 'Nah, bro.' How is that better?
jokes.setup: Executive offers alternative to saying no
jokes.punchline: Ben points out the obvious flaw in the logic
jokes.explanation: Good followup that highlights corporate doublespeak absurdity

jokes.text: Who do you think I am, Thomas Hucker?
jokes.setup: Tom wants chairs rushed
jokes.punchline: Ron references an obscure furniture maker as if everyone should know
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reference to obscure craftsmanship knowledge

jokes.text: Please, Ron? I've never asked you for anything today.
jokes.setup: Tom pleads with Ron for help
jokes.punchline: Absurd timeframe for claiming he's never asked for anything
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic typical of Tom's self-serving reasoning

jokes.text: I cut out your face and made it look like you were dunking over Charles Barkley.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions making a poster of Rebecca
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific detail about the poster
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd detail that perfectly captures Leslie's government nerdiness

jokes.text: It wasn't bad.
jokes.setup: Rebecca praises Leslie's proposal highly
jokes.punchline: Liam gives the most minimal possible compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing bureaucratic understatement

jokes.text: Throughout history. Until the end of time, forever.
jokes.setup: Leslie agrees with Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd levels of agreement across all time
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's fangirling getting out of control

jokes.text: Great work! I'm sorry I'm talking so loud! I'm getting louder and now I can't stop! My apologies!
jokes.setup: Leslie is nervous meeting Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Her volume spirals out of control and she can't stop it
jokes.explanation: Great escalating cringe moment that's very relatable

jokes.text: I thought he was gonna be on it.
jokes.setup: They watch something go down a hill fast
jokes.punchline: Andy reveals he expected Ben to ride whatever went down
jokes.explanation: Good Andy logic about expecting dangerous stunts

jokes.text: I don't know! Liam Bonneville has me in a tizzy!
jokes.setup: Ben asks why she didn't mention Obama first
jokes.punchline: Leslie is more excited about a random bureaucrat
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that escalates Leslie's government nerdiness

jokes.text: No. Can't I just stay in the back and spit in people's food like we planned?
jokes.setup: Tom asks April to handle service
jokes.punchline: She prefers the previously planned food contamination
jokes.explanation: Perfect April darkness in a way that's character-specific

jokes.text: My sinuses are on the fritz, and smell is 90% of taste! Maybe 95!
jokes.setup: Craig complains about sawdust affecting his sinuses
jokes.punchline: Escalates the percentage of taste that's smell
jokes.explanation: Good Craig intensity and escalation

jokes.text: Fine, but if he raises his voice once, once, I'm out.
jokes.setup: Tom assigns Donna to work with Craig
jokes.punchline: She sets a very specific limit knowing Craig's tendencies
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Craig's inevitable outburst

jokes.text: Please don't stick to that policy.
jokes.setup: Donna says she'll leave if Craig raises his voice once
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately recognizes this policy won't work
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat acknowledging the obvious

jokes.text: What is this, a rotten grapefruit? No, it's my dog's rectum.
jokes.setup: Tom sees disgusting pictures on the menus
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals it's his dog's medical photos
jokes.explanation: Excellent gross-out comedy with perfect Larry incompetence

jokes.text: This is the worst thing you've ever done.
jokes.setup: Larry has messed up restaurant menus badly
jokes.punchline: Tom declares this Larry's worst mistake ever
jokes.explanation: Good escalation considering Larry's track record

jokes.text: It was too perfect. It looked machine-made.
jokes.setup: Tom asks what was wrong with a chair
jokes.punchline: Ron's impossible craftsmanship standards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment showing his artisan philosophy taken to extremes

jokes.text: we could tell them that there's buried treasure in Pawnee, and then, after they give us the free WiFi, we tell them that the treasure was love all along.
jokes.setup: Andy suggests convincing Gryzzl that Pawnee is special
jokes.punchline: Elaborate scheme ending with cheesy revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy plan that's simultaneously sweet and absurd

jokes.text: Every time someone in Pawnee clicks through a slideshow of American Music Award red carpet sideboob fails, they'll say, 'Thank you, Ben Wyatt.'
jokes.setup: Ben wants to give Pawnee free WiFi as a going-away present
jokes.punchline: Imagines being thanked for enabling trashy internet browsing
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail about how people would actually use free WiFi

jokes.text: You're The Architect? Yeah, right. And I'm the alchemist of the Hinterlands.
jokes.setup: Ben claims to be The Architect
jokes.punchline: Employee sarcastically claims equally impressive game title
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response showing disbelief

jokes.text: There can't be an alchemist of the Hinterlands. The Hinterlands is a shadow kingdom that can only sustain a provost or a denier.
jokes.setup: Employee claims to be alchemist of the Hinterlands
jokes.punchline: Ben corrects him with absurdly specific game rules
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ben nerdiness moment with perfect game creator authority

jokes.text: Neither. I'm the maverick.
jokes.setup: Asked to choose between corporal or warrior
jokes.punchline: Ben chooses the most Ben-like game piece
jokes.explanation: Perfect character choice showing Ben's self-image

jokes.text: You lost your cityhood?
jokes.setup: Rebecca mentions unincorporated territory
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horror at the concept
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie reaction to government failure

jokes.text: Which is it? Fruity or earthy? It can't be both, they're mutually exclusive!
jokes.setup: Donna describes wine as fruity and earthy
jokes.punchline: Craig's intense perfectionism about wine description
jokes.explanation: Good Craig intensity about something trivial

jokes.text: I don't know who I am anymore. A man without a palate isn't a man.
jokes.setup: Craig is upset about his compromised taste
jokes.punchline: Escalates to existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Craig escalation to philosophical crisis over minor issue

jokes.text: Restaurant superstition, breaking a glass on the first night is good luck.
jokes.setup: Someone breaks a glass
jokes.punchline: Tom makes up superstition on the spot
jokes.explanation: Good Tom spin control and improvisation

jokes.text: It's about the cones.
jokes.setup: Ben explains the opponent's mistake
jokes.punchline: Reveals the deep strategy is actually very simple
jokes.explanation: Brilliant distillation of Ben's game philosophy into perfect economy

jokes.text: We had to throw our menus away because they are covered with pictures of Larry's dog's rectum.
jokes.setup: Customer asks for menu
jokes.punchline: April matter-of-factly explains the gross situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery of absurd information

jokes.text: We just have to apply a salve to his anus every hour for the rest of his life, but... Oh! Enjoy your meal!
jokes.setup: Larry updates on his dog's medical condition
jokes.punchline: Ends with cheerful meal wish after gross details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obliviousness creating maximum cringe

jokes.text: I mean, people are playing with my cones, babe.
jokes.setup: Ben describes his game's popularity
jokes.punchline: Phrase that sounds sexual but isn't
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo from Ben's innocence

jokes.text: I'm gonna be paralyzed by hypotheticals until I die here, in this mini van! At the San Francisco airport.
jokes.setup: Leslie can't decide about the job
jokes.punchline: Escalates to dying in specific mundane location
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie anxiety escalation with specific detail

jokes.text: When I was a kid and things didn't go my way, I would just take my ball and go home. That's better than winning, 'cause then, your friends can't play anymore!
jokes.setup: Tom talks about loving to quit
jokes.punchline: Reveals his childhood strategy was spite-based
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom psychology - finding victory in petty revenge

jokes.text: Well, I hate doing work, but I love being flattered, so maybe I'll give it another try.
jokes.setup: April gives Tom a pep talk
jokes.punchline: Tom's honest cost-benefit analysis of motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom honesty about his motivations

jokes.text: Because you currently owe me $16,000. For the wood. What?
jokes.setup: Ron gives Tom a pep talk
jokes.punchline: Reveals massive debt for materials
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection from supportive to business transaction

jokes.text: Sorry, that's not why we're here. Let's go.
jokes.setup: Andy gets excited about Star Wars forest
jokes.punchline: Ben dismisses it matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Standard straight-man response to Andy enthusiasm

jokes.text: I love you. No, I don't. I'm sorry. That was weird. Okay, bye.
jokes.setup: Leslie calls Grant about the job
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says 'I love you' then awkwardly backpedals
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment of phone call anxiety

jokes.text: Oh, this is Lester Kanopf. No, you know... Okay. Bye. Smoothly handled, Lester. Yeah. I panicked.
jokes.setup: Leslie is flustered on the phone
jokes.punchline: Calls Grant by wrong name, then admits panic
jokes.explanation: Good callback with continued phone anxiety

jokes.text: All right, so we got a four-way going with my ex, my brother, and this rando? Okay, if that's where the night is going.
jokes.setup: Tom mentions needing help from multiple people
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio immediately assumes sexual scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio assumption about any group activity

jokes.text: It's 9:00 in the morning. What? It's not a four-way.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio assumes sexual scenario
jokes.punchline: Tom points out it's morning and clarifies
jokes.explanation: Standard straight-man correction

jokes.text: playing music is something I like to keep private, along with my family, my conversations, and my whereabouts at all times.
jokes.setup: Andy asks Ron to perform music
jokes.punchline: Ron lists everything he keeps private (basically everything)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron privacy philosophy escalation

jokes.text: You're right, the name is too long, I was picturing bigger hats.
jokes.setup: Andy mentions the name is too long
jokes.punchline: Leslie's random concern about hat size accommodation
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie non sequitur about practical concerns

jokes.text: Only a moron would ever live anywhere other than Pawnee, Indiana. Leslie Knope, multiple occasions.
jokes.setup: Ron reads the statue inscription
jokes.punchline: Quote is ironic given Leslie is moving
jokes.explanation: Good ironic inscription that comments on Leslie's decision

jokes.text: Leslie, don't cry. It's not that great. Doesn't even look like you. Or actually, you know what? It kinda does.
jokes.setup: April tries to comfort crying Leslie
jokes.punchline: Backtracks from insult to accidental insult
jokes.explanation: Good April attempt at comfort that becomes worse

jokes.text: I ordered a small cheeseburger and both the buns were pizzas.
jokes.setup: Band comments on Pawnee being amazing
jokes.punchline: Absurd food combination as evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Pawnee food innovation

jokes.text: Judge gave me two ankle bracelets. Said it was the first time he ever had to do that. Ja boy's a question on the bar exam! Ya heard!
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio mentions house arrest
jokes.punchline: Escalates to unprecedented legal situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jean-Ralphio moment turning legal precedent into bragging rights

jokes.text: Unless you want me to get on stage and talk about how we used to take baths together. How you cried whenever I took away your rubber duckies. Damn, Donna, why you gotta bring the Quackson Five into this?
jokes.setup: Donna threatens to embarrass Ginuwine
jokes.punchline: He named his rubber duckies 'The Quackson Five'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun name for rubber ducks that's perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: No, just trolling for some daddy. Sorry, I pronounced that wrong. I'm trolling for some dad-dy.
jokes.setup: Tammy explains why she's at children's event
jokes.punchline: Clarifies sexual pronunciation of 'daddy'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tammy inappropriate behavior with pronunciation joke

jokes.text: And I will defeat you right into my pants.
jokes.setup: Tammy threatens to defeat Ron
jokes.punchline: Sexualizes the concept of defeat
jokes.explanation: Standard Tammy sexual aggression

jokes.text: What? I gave a kid pee-pants? Yes! That's why I do what I do.
jokes.setup: April tells Andy a kid peed from excitement
jokes.punchline: Andy treats this as the highest accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy finding pride in childish metrics

jokes.text: but I like to do nothing with no one. So, thank you and I love you, but no thank you, and I hate you. More I love you.
jokes.setup: April explains why she won't move to Chicago
jokes.punchline: Contradictory emotional statements
jokes.explanation: Perfect April emotional confusion and honesty

jokes.text: No! Your father ruined my last business. Why would you bring him here? Mmm, because you told me to look for VIPs and my daddy is the Vl-est PI know.
jokes.setup: Tom is upset Mona-Lisa brought her father
jokes.punchline: She explains daddy is 'VIest P' (Very Important Person)
jokes.explanation: Good Mona-Lisa wordplay and family loyalty

jokes.text: 'Cause there's nobody here.
jokes.setup: Saperstein asks if he needs reservation
jokes.punchline: Points out restaurant is empty
jokes.explanation: Brutal but accurate observation

jokes.text: I'll have the 'Joan.' It's a tumbler of gin and it's got crushed aspirin around the rim.
jokes.setup: Joan orders a drink named after herself
jokes.punchline: It's dangerously medicinal cocktail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joan Callamezzo cocktail reflecting her lifestyle

jokes.text: Come work for us at the accounting firm? Okay. No, sorry, I can't. Wow. Shortest one yet.
jokes.setup: Barney offers Ben a job
jokes.punchline: Ben's record-fast rejection
jokes.explanation: Good running gag about Ben rejecting accounting jobs

jokes.text: And I've been to Dallas.
jokes.setup: Saperstein praises Italian meal
jokes.punchline: His credential is having been to Dallas
jokes.explanation: Good Saperstein logic treating Dallas as Italian food authority

jokes.text: Excuse me, Miss Hanley? Do you mind if I snap a 'You-ie'? It's what I call selfies of other people.
jokes.setup: Tom wants photo with Kay Hanley
jokes.punchline: Made-up term 'You-ie' for photos of others
jokes.explanation: Good Tom invention of silly terminology

jokes.text: With your courage and small frame, you'd make an excellent coal miner.
jokes.setup: Ron suggests Leslie use her talents elsewhere
jokes.punchline: Suggests dangerous job based on physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron career advice that's both practical and inappropriate

jokes.text: Also, can you bring back Power Rangers? I don't know what it is you do, but you seem important enough to get that done.
jokes.setup: Andy addresses Grant about government influence
jokes.punchline: Assumes federal employee can revive TV show
jokes.explanation: Good Andy confusion about government powers

jokes.text: Ed, you're a nice guy, but you're the most incompetent person I've ever worked with. And that includes Terry. Jeez. Get it together, Terry.
jokes.setup: Leslie fires Ed for incompetence
jokes.punchline: Compares to Terry, who gets defensive
jokes.explanation: Standard running gag about government incompetence

jokes.text: Ben, how many bags of marshmallows do I give them? None. Just let April do everything.
jokes.setup: Andy asks about feeding kids
jokes.punchline: Ben's parental wisdom about letting April handle it
jokes.explanation: Good parenting wisdom about trusting competent people
